task_completion.description: The DICOM viewer application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of a DICOM viewer application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A DICOM image is loaded and displayed in the viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A medical image (X-Ray Angiography) is visible within the viewing area of the DICOM application.

task_completion.description: The Lens (magnifying glass) tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Lens or magnifying glass icon in the toolbar is highlighted, pressed, or selected.

task_completion.description: The active lens overlay is visible on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A magnifying glass overlay (usually a circular or rectangular area showing a zoomed-in portion of the image) is actively displayed on top of the DICOM image.
A terminal window, file manager, or save dialog shows the file 'lens_inspection.png' successfully created or saved in the '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real DICOM viewer GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual interface of a DICOM viewer, not a text editor, web browser showing a static image, or image manipulation software.

integrity.description: The lens overlay shows a genuine magnification effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: The area inside the lens overlay correctly shows a magnified view of the underlying micro-structures, rather than being a pasted or drawn circle.

integrity.description: The screenshot file was genuinely captured and not faked via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using commands like 'touch', 'echo', or 'wget' to create a dummy file at the target path; the file should be the result of a screenshot action.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output screenshot must be saved at '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/lens_inspection.png' and have a minimum file size of 50 KB. The underlying images are X-Ray Angiography (XA) DICOM samples.
